Festivals & Holidays

Purnima Days

Eclipses in 2026 (Grahan)
Solar and Lunar eclipses with visibility in India
Total Lunar Eclipse

March 14, 2026

Visible in India

Partial Solar Eclipse

March 29, 2026

Not visible in India

Total Lunar Eclipse

September 7, 2026

Visible in India

Annular Solar Eclipse

September 22, 2026

January 2026

Lohri

Regional

Punjabi harvest festival celebrated with bonfires

January 13, 2026

Makar Sankranti

Major

Sun enters Capricorn, kite flying festival

January 14, 2026

Pongal

Regional

Tamil harvest festival spanning four days

January 14-17, 2026

Republic Day

National

National holiday celebrating Indian constitution

January 26, 2026

Basant Panchami

Major

Worship of Goddess Saraswati, arrival of spring

January 29, 2026

Maha Shivaratri

Major

Night of Lord Shiva, fasting and night vigil

February 26, 2026
March 2026

Holika Dahan

Major

Burning of Holika, victory of good over evil

March 13, 2026

Holi

Major

Festival of colors celebrating spring

March 14, 2026

Chaitra Navratri Begins

Major

Nine nights of Goddess Durga worship

March 29, 2026
April 2026

Ugadi / Gudi Padwa

Regional

Telugu and Marathi New Year

Ram Navami

Major

Birth anniversary of Lord Rama

Major

Auspicious day for new beginnings and gold purchase

Regional

Descent of River Ganga to Earth

Nirjala Ekadashi

Vrat

Strictest Ekadashi fast without water

Major

Day to honor spiritual teachers

Eid ul-Adha

Worship of serpent deities

Krishna Janmashtami

Major

Birth anniversary of Lord Krishna

Ganesh Chaturthi

Anant Chaturdashi

Major

Ganesh Visarjan, worship of Lord Vishnu

Onam

Regional

Kerala harvest festival

September 4, 2026

Vrat

September 7, 2026

Sharad Navratri Begins

Major

Nine nights of Goddess Durga worship

Durga Ashtami

Major

Maha Navami

Major

Ninth day of Navratri

Dussehra / Vijayadashami

Major

Karwa Chauth

Vrat

Dhanteras

Major

First day of Diwali, worship of Lord Dhanvantari

Narak Chaturdashi

Major

Second day of Diwali, Choti Diwali

Diwali

Major

Festival of lights, worship of Lakshmi and Ganesha

October 20, 2026

Govardhan Puja

Major

Day after Diwali, worship of Govardhan Hill

October 21, 2026

Bhai Dooj

Major

Sisters pray for brothers' well-being

Chhath Puja

Regional

Sun worship festival, mainly in Bihar

Diwali of the Gods, celebrated in Varanasi

December 1, 2026

December 25, 2026

About Hindu Festival Calendar

The Hindu festival calendar follows the lunisolar system, combining both lunar and solar movements to determine dates. Most Hindu festivals are based on the Panchang, which considers Tithi (lunar day), Nakshatra (lunar mansion), and other astronomical factors.

Major festivals like Diwali, Holi, Navratri, and Ganesh Chaturthi are celebrated across India, while regional festivals like Pongal, Onam, and Baisakhi have special significance in specific states. The calendar also includes important fasting days (Vrat) like Ekadashi, Pradosh, and Karwa Chauth.

Eclipses (Grahan) hold special significance in Hindu tradition. During eclipses, temples are closed, and specific rituals are performed. The Sutak period before and after eclipses is considered inauspicious for starting new activities.
